title: DeploymentBootstrapOptions
description: Configuration for the real, deployment-agnostic bootstrap of an Arazzo control plane (design §14/§16). Every value that is specific to a deployment lives here, so the bootstrap logic itself is generic. Supplied as JSON and validated against this schema, so a deployment (ZeroFailed, appsettings, a secret store) configures the control plane declaratively rather than through hand-authored code.

identityClaimType.description: The token claim carrying a principal's group/identity memberships (e.g. 'groups'). Used for the genesis-administrator grant and as the source of the sys: identity dimension (§16.5.5).

genesisAdminGroup.description: The value of identityClaimType granted genesis administration (§16.2 tier 3): all capability scopes plus unrestricted read/write/purge reach. The first administrator logs in via OIDC already holding admin — bootstrapped by config, the identity analogue of secret-zero. This stored binding IS the deliberate deployment policy; no group otherwise confers capability by mere membership.

genesisScopes.description: The capability scopes the genesis administrator holds (e.g. 'catalog:read', 'runs:write', 'security:write'). A deployment typically supplies the full control-plane scope set.

genesisAdditionalClauses.description: Extra identity-dimension clauses (design §16.5.4) ANDed with the genesis administrator's primary group clause to form a tag-set selector: the genesis grant then applies only to a caller whose canonical identity also carries every one of these dimensions. Use it to issuer-qualify the genesis grant (dimension 'iss' = the deployment's IdP), so a group of the same name asserted by a different identity provider does not inherit genesis administration. Absent is a single-clause group grant.

dimension.description: The operator-facing identity dimension (the internal prefix stripped) the caller must carry.

value.description: The value the dimension must equal; absent matches any value of the dimension.

labelOrderings.description: The ordered tag dimensions (§14.2): each property is a dimension name mapping to its labels in ascending order (e.g. classification -> [public, internal, confidential, restricted]). The ordered rule templates rank against these; surfaced read-only at GET /security/orderings.

internalTagPrefix.description: The reserved prefix for deployment-internal, client-invisible security tags (§14.3), e.g. 'sys:'.

selfElevationGroups.description: Groups whose members may JIT self-elevate without a human approver (§16.5.3). Everyone else submits an access request approved by a workflow administrator.

seedExampleData.description: When true, the deployment additionally runs its IExampleSeed (demo/fixture content: sample workflows, personas, credential references). Production deployments leave this false.

systemWorkflows.description: When present, the deployment installs the bootstrapped access-approval system workflow (design §16.5.1) so Arazzo governs its own access approvals: the workflow package is catalogued and made available in an internal environment, and the system runner's OAuth2 client-credentials identity is provisioned. Absent means the deployment runs approvals through the built-in direct-to-administrator strategy instead. The workflow's §15 administrator is the genesis administrator, and the credential's usage is scoped to the workflow's own identity, so only its runs may present it.

tokenUrl.description: The OAuth2 token endpoint the system runner's client-credentials grant is fetched from (e.g. the IdP's .../protocol/openid-connect/token). The runner presents the issued token, carrying the accessRequests:grant scope, when calling grantAccessRequest.

clientId.description: The OAuth2 client id the system runner presents — the IdP client whose issued token carries the accessRequests:grant scope.

brokerUrl.description: The message broker endpoint the system approval workflow's notification channels ride in the internal environment (the access-notifications channel credential's serverUrl config, ADR 0051). Optional: absent means the deployment binds the channel credential itself.

brokerTokenRef.description: The secret reference (scheme://locator[#field]) resolving the broker connection token (the bearer channel-credential shape, presented in the CONNECT handshake); required when brokerUrl is set.

clientSecretRef.description: The secret reference (scheme://locator[#field]) resolving the runner client's secret; a pointer into the external secret store, never the secret itself.

environment.description: The control-plane internal environment the approval workflow executes and is made available in (served by the system runner).
